ourQualities.title: FARM SOURCED
ourQualities.description: Every ingredient traced to local farms within 50 miles. We know our farmers by name and visit their fields weekly.

ourQualities.title: WOOD FIRED
ourQualities.description: Our kitchen centers around a hand-built brick oven. Smoky, charred, alive — flavors you can't replicate on gas.

ourQualities.title: MADE TODAY
ourQualities.description: No freezers, no shortcuts. What doesn't sell by closing feeds our staff family meal or composts back to those farms.

dishes.title: Lamb Rump with Rosemary
dishes.description: Grass-fed Dorset lamb, slow-roasted over oak with garlic and wild rosemary. Served with parsnip purée and charred spring onions.
dishes.chefNote: We get these lambs from the Henderson farm. They graze on thyme-covered hillsides — you can taste it in the meat.

dishes.title: Citrus Cured Salmon
dishes.description: Scottish salmon cured for 48 hours in lemon, dill, and juniper. Paper-thin slices with rye crisps and horseradish cream.
dishes.chefNote: The cure draws out moisture while the citrus 'cooks' the fish. Texture should be like silk, not raw.

dishes.title: Sea Bass with Fennel
dishes.description: Line-caught bass, pan-seared until skin crackles. Served on braised fennel with saffron broth and roasted cherry tomatoes.
dishes.chefNote: The skin is everything. Score it deep, dry it well, press it down hard in a hot pan. Listen for the crackle.

dishes.title: Honey Roasted Figs
dishes.description: Black mission figs roasted with thyme honey and mascarpone. Finished with toasted walnuts and aged balsamic.
dishes.chefNote: September through October only. When the figs run out, this comes off the menu. No compromises.

dishes.title: Brioche Burger
dishes.description: Dry-aged beef patty, caramelized onion jam, and clothbound cheddar. House-made brioche baked every morning.
dishes.chefNote: The onion jam takes three hours. We start it before dawn, before the bread goes in the oven.

dishes.title: Mussels in Cider Broth
dishes.description: Pembrokeshire mussels steamed in dry cider with shallots, parsley, and cultured butter. Grilled sourdough for soaking.
dishes.chefNote: If a mussel doesn't close when tapped, it goes in the bin. No exceptions. The broth should taste like the sea.

dishes.title: Hand-Rolled Pappardelle
dishes.description: Wide ribbons of pasta with slow-braised beef shin ragu. Parmesan aged 24 months, grated tableside.
dishes.chefNote: My grandmother's recipe. The ragu cooks for six hours until the meat falls apart with a spoon.

dishes.title: Whole Grilled Sea Bream
dishes.description: Mediterranean sea bream grilled whole with lemon, capers, and olive oil. Served with charred broccoli and aioli.
dishes.chefNote: We score the fish deeply so the heat penetrates evenly. The flesh near the bone should be just translucent.

dishes.title: Wild Mushroom Risotto
dishes.description: Creamy risotto with wild mushrooms, truffle oil, and parmesan. Served with a side of seasonal greens.
dishes.chefNote: The key is to stir constantly and add the stock gradually. The rice should be creamy but still have a slight bite.

team.name: Marcus Chen
team.bio: Trained at Le Cordon Bleu, then spent five years in Lyon. Opened Savour & Stone in 2019 after realizing he missed the produce of his Yorkshire childhood.
team.quote: Technique gets you to good. Patience gets you to great.

team.name: Elena Vasquez
team.bio: Born in Barcelona, raised in Manchester. Specializes in our wood-fired program and makes the best paella you've had outside Valencia.
team.quote: Fire is alive. You don't control it — you negotiate with it.

team.name: James O'Brien
team.bio: Formerly of The Fat Duck. Now he keeps it simple: seasonal fruit, good butter, and sugar that doesn't mask flavor.
team.quote: Dessert should end the meal, not overwhelm it.

team.name: Sarah Kim
team.bio: Wine sommelier with a memory for faces. She remembers your anniversary, your allergies, and exactly how you like your martini.
team.quote: Hospitality is making people feel at home, even when they've never been here before.
